summary: Iter 20 — Full backend + frontend verification of the new Wayly Admin suite (/api/admin/* + /admin/* routes). 27/27 pytest cases pass: admin login returns is_admin=true, analytics shape correct, users list + search + detail + 404, households/payments/statements lists (no file_b64/raw_text/_id leakage), 3 CSV exports, admin self-protection (cannot demote self → 400, cannot delete self → 400), toggle-admin and set-plan persist via GET re-fetch, invalid plan → 400. RBAC verified: unauthenticated → 401 on /api/admin/analytics; non-admin (cathy) → 403 on all 8 admin paths tested. Frontend Playwright: admin login lands on /app with Admin sidebar nav-admin link visible, /admin Overview renders admin-analytics container with 7 stat panels and all expected section labels (Total users / Households / Statements decoded / Revenue / Plans / Subscriptions / Top active households). Users tab shows 25 rows, search 'cathy' narrows to 1, user drawer opens with all 4 admin-action testids present, CSV export links rendered. Households / Payments / Statements tabs each render their respective testid container with their export link. Non-admin (cathy) sidebar lacks nav-admin link and /admin navigation redirects to /app with the 'Admin access required' sonner toast (observed twice — once per nav). After re-login as admin, nav-admin link reappears. No backend mutations leaked; cleanup restored target user's plan and is_admin flag.

issues: React console warning: 'Encountered two children with the same key' fires multiple times (~25) when /app Dashboard mounts. Not from AdminApp.jsx — admin tables all key on unique IDs. Likely from an array-mapped component on Dashboard/Layout. Non-blocking for admin functionality but worth fixing to avoid noise.

action_items: (Optional) Track down the duplicate-key React warning that fires on the /app Dashboard mount — not in AdminApp.jsx; likely a sibling component rendered before Admin nav (Settings/Layout/Dashboard panels). Doesn't affect admin behaviour.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_routes.py is clean: every endpoint depends on get_current_admin_id, _id is always projected out, password_hash is stripped from both list+detail and CSV export. Heavy fields (file_b64, raw_text, audit, line_items) projected out of statements list. Good.
Self-protection guards are correctly placed in toggle_admin and admin_delete_user (compare user_id == admin_id BEFORE any DB write). Verified by tests.

auth.py get_current_admin_id creates a new AsyncIOMotorClient per request (lines 72-73). This works but is sub-optimal — each admin call opens a fresh Mongo connection pool. Recommend lazy-cache a module-level client. Not blocking, but will matter under load.

AdminApp.jsx (~600 lines, single file) is OK as a route module today, but if Households/Payments/Statements grow detail-drawers or filters, this file will pass the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting per-route components into separate files once any one grows beyond ~200 lines.
AdminUsers' UserDetailDrawer.act() optimistically re-fetches detail + calls onMutate (parent re-list). Good — no stale UI after toggle-admin/set-plan/cancel.
Drawer 'Delete user' click uses window.confirm — fine for an internal admin tool but consider replacing with the AlertDialog shadcn primitive for visual consistency with the rest of Wayly.
Layout.jsx renders the admin sidebar item only when user.is_admin is truthy (line 118-120). Confirmed by Playwright: cathy lacks the link, admin sees it.

RequireAdmin in AdminApp.jsx waits for AuthContext.loading before deciding — prevents flicker. Good. Toast 'Admin access required' fires inside useEffect — observed twice in our test (React StrictMode double-invoke in dev). Harmless but ToastService dedup could be considered.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on admin suite — login, all 5 tabs, drawer, CSV links, RBAC redirect with toast, sidebar visibility all verified

test_credentials: Admin: hello@techglove.com.au / AdminPass!2026 (promoted via seed_admin.py — re-ran to ensure flag); Non-admin: cathy@example.com / testpass123
seed_data_creation: Re-ran /app/backend/seed_admin.py once at start to ensure hello@techglove.com.au has is_admin=true. Script is idempotent (output: 'Existing user … promoted to admin.'). No other seed data created. test_toggle_admin_on_another_user and test_set_plan_valid_and_persists mutate the cathy@example.com record but both restore the original values in a finally/cleanup block.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin suite is fully live and passing. Reusable test files: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_suite.py (pytest, 27 cases, ~3s). To re-run: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_admin_suite.py -v. For frontend, hit /admin while logged in as hello@techglove.com.au; testids follow the pattern admin-analytics, admin-users, admin-user-row-{id}, admin-user-drawer, admin-action-{reset-password|toggle-admin|set-plan|cancel-sub|delete-user}, admin-export-{users|payments|statements}-csv, admin-nav-{analytics|users|households|payments|statements}, and nav-admin on the main Layout sidebar. The seed_admin.py script is safe to re-run any time. AdminApp.jsx lives at /app/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminApp.jsx; backend admin code at /app/backend/admin_routes.py.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no issues found in this iteration.
